idea-plugin.name: JBeans
idea-plugin.description:
Invoke any Spring Bean method directly from your IDE — no test code, no curl, no Postman.

JBeans uses the JVM Attach API to connect to your running Spring Boot process, injects a lightweight in-memory agent, and lets you call any Bean method, static utility method, or HTTP endpoint through a clean tool window UI.

Features

  • Bean Invocation — Browse Spring Beans by name or class, edit JSON parameters, invoke, and see the serialized result instantly.
  • Static Method Support — Call any public static method on any class in your project, with the same JSON parameter editor.
  • HTTP Testing — Scan @RequestMapping routes; auto-fill path variables, query params, and request body; send requests and view the raw response.
  • Call History — Every invocation is persisted across IDE restarts; replay or star entries for quick access.
  • Zero Config — No agents to pre-install, no changes to your application code or build scripts.

Requirements

  • IntelliJ IDEA 2023.2 or later
  • Java 8+ target JVM
  • Spring Boot application running locally (any version)
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.0 — Initial release
  • Bean method invocation via JVM Attach + in-memory agent
  • Static method invocation support
  • HTTP route testing with auto-filled parameters
  • Call history with star and replay
  • JSON validation and cancel support
